title: eragrostis betsileensis
intro: Eragrostis betsileensis, also known as Madagascar grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its medicinal properties. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help reduce pain and swelling, as well as combat infections. In herbal preparations, Eragrostis betsileensis is often made into a tea or infused in oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
